name: Sweet Potato Tagine
recipeIngredient: 14 1/2 ounces low-sodium fat-free chicken broth, or vegetable broth
1/2 ounce dried porcini mushrooms, coarsely chopped
4 sweet potatoes, about 2 pounds (peeled & cut into chunks)
2 cups cooked couscous, Israeli or Moroccan
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. In an oven-proof casserole, combine broth and mushrooms; bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 2 minutes.
2. Remove the casserole from heat and add sweet potatoes, dried tomatoes, sage, garlic, and salt. Cover and bake in the oven for 30 minutes, stirring once.
3. Add the olive oil to the sweet-potato mixture; stir and bake, uncovered, for an additional 15 minutes. Stir well and let cool for at least 5 minutes before serving, allowing liquid to thicken slightly. Serve over couscous.
Notes: Sweet potatoes contain more than 4 times the recommended daily allowance of beta-carotene (more than carrots!), which protects against cancer and heart disease. The tuber also contains high levels of vitamins C and E, which bolster the immune system. Other nutrient bonuses include plenty of vitamin A, potassium, iron, B vitamins, and fiber. Despite their rich taste, these potatoes are fat- and cholesterol-free; a medium sweet potato has only about 130 calories.
